summary.intent: Integrate cargo-crap CRAP metric tool into xtask and CI with baseline/regression mode, and speed up CI by removing the redundant check job and rewiring the dependency graph.
summary.outcome: cargo-crap integrated with baseline/regression mode (--fail-regression --min 30) rather than hard-fail absolute threshold, due to ~24 Machine::transition functions with CC 31-77 that are well-tested. CI improved: check job removed, fan-out rewired from needs:[unit] to needs:[clippy], native coverage added to xci-fast. PR #700 opened. Codex found P1 bug: baseline had absolute paths making the gate a silent no-op on CI. Fix was in progress at transcript end.

repo: xtask uses a Step enum pattern for CI orchestration; mutants.rs is the established pattern for external tool preflight checks and cargo subcommand invocation
CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md, which is the canonical file
spec/testing/14-ci.md is the authoritative CI spec and must be kept in sync with ci.yml changes
ars-derive is a proc-macro intentionally unmeasured; ars-e2e is not coverage-gated; 15 crates have coverage thresholds

The codebase has ~24 Machine::transition functions (one per stateful component) with CC 31-77 that are 90-100% tested — an absolute CRAP threshold of 30 would fight the architecture permanently as new components are added
New functions under --fail-regression are categorized as new (not regressed), so future stateful components do not trigger the gate
--exclude is a silent no-op under --workspace in cargo-crap; --allow works instead
--path . emits repo-relative paths (./crates/...) while --workspace gives absolute machine paths — critical for CI portability since baseline keys on (file, function, line)
--fail-regression alone fails on any CRAP increase including sub-threshold; --min 30 narrows it to only above-threshold regressions
LLVM-22 apt install takes 1-3 min in the coverage job; dioxus-desktop-deps composite action is used by 6 jobs
Clippy 1.96 (released 2026-05-25) added unnecessary_option_map_or_else as deny-by-default under the suspicious group, breaking main for everyone

code.finding: run_coverage produces merged lcov.info at repo root; cargo-crap consumes this with zero recompile
Pre-existing clippy-1.96 unnecessary_option_map_or_else lint: map_or_else(|| clamp_page(...), identity) should be unwrap_or_else(|| clamp_page(...))
check_all treats packages with lines_found == 0 as SKIP (not FAIL), so a native-subset threshold list is needed to avoid false failures for wasm-only crates in native coverage runs

New file: three modes (Gate/Report/UpdateBaseline), uses --path . not --workspace, --exclude (works under --path, unlike --workspace), bootstrap-if-missing baseline. Guard test verifies committed baseline has no absolute paths.

workflow: cargo-crap --fail-regression --min 30 is the right gate for mature codebases: ignores sub-threshold churn, catches above-threshold regressions, tolerates new functions entirely
Baseline files must use relative paths for CI portability; add a guard test that reads the committed baseline file and asserts no absolute paths
Background cargo commands stall on build-dir locks when run concurrently — run one at a time and wait for completion
PIPESTATUS[0] after a pipe (cmd | tail) captures tail's exit code not the main command's — capture exit code before piping
git reset --soft HEAD~1 followed by commit --amend can accidentally bundle unrelated commits; use ORIG_HEAD or an explicit SHA to recover the lost tip
A background task notification reporting exit code 0 may reflect the shell wrapper, not the actual tool — always verify the log file

friction: cargo-crap --exclude is silently ignored under --workspace with no warning; had to discover via empirical testing by comparing output with and without the flag
Committed baseline had absolute /Users/ericson/... paths, making the regression gate a complete silent no-op on CI — caught only by Codex review (P1)
Stale gitignored lcov.info (1 month old) gave misleading calibration data: 481 over-threshold functions vs actual ~200 from a fresh run
Background xci-fast run stalled on build-dir lock from a concurrent cargo invocation, producing an empty output file and a false exit-0 report
PIPESTATUS masking: task notification reported exit 0, hiding a real clippy failure until the log was read directly
Accidental git reset --soft HEAD~1 bundled all cargo-crap files into an unrelated navigation-audit commit via implicit amend
Clippy 1.96 unnecessary_option_map_or_else lint broke all of main on 2026-05-25; surfaced by xci-fast pre-push gate and required an unplanned fix commit
Baseline regeneration required multiple iterations: stale lcov → workspace-absolute paths → path-relative + examples excluded
open_items: Branch protection: must manually remove Workspace Check required status check in GitHub settings after merge or merges will hang
Flaky ars-dom instrumented wasm test (CI Failure A) — separate triage/quarantine investigation deferred
.crap-baseline.json is 2.4 MB; could reduce with --min 15 (~10x smaller) at cost of missing single-PR jumps from far-below to above threshold
Pre-existing stale ASCII dependency graph in spec/testing/14-ci.md uses node names like core-tests that never matched real jobs — deferred as out-of-scope for this PR
kache (RUSTC_WRAPPER content-addressed Rust artifact cache) — deferred to a separate benchmark spike on its own branch
Custom CI image with LLVM-22 preinstalled — deferred
PR-native-only / nightly-full coverage split to reduce coverage job frequency on PRs — deferred
Codex P1 fix (--path . relative paths) committed but not yet pushed at transcript end; needs push and @codex review re-trigger
